transcript: Dear fellow astronomers, as we peer into deep space we look back on our lonely planet full of life and must acknowledge our role in protecting it. We are scientists, educators, engaged citizens and now changemakers. Already our field is being challenged like never before. Studies in the Atacama Desert revealed the declining quality of ground-based astronomical observations as climate change alters weather patterns. Meanwhile, our fossil fuel powered observatories, supercomputers and international conferences give us an unsustainable carbon footprint. We must convince our organizations to invest in renewable energy sources and adopt sustainable practices throughout our institutions. Astronomers also have a unique reach. We can harness public interest in our field to highlight the urgent need for action and be global leaders by making our disciplines sustainable. We can lead the way to a sustainable future. Join us. NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory, California Institute of Technology
